title: Senior Ride Programs - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Senior Ride Programs 17 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter African American Community Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CLACKAMAS COUNTY SOCIAL SERVICES TRANSPORTATION REACHING PEOPLE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides transportation for medical appointments, personal business, and shopping. Priority is given to life-sustaining medical appointments. Rides are provided within Clackamas, Multnomah, and Washington Counties. The Ride Together program allows riders to recruit their own volunteer driver (i.e., family, neighbors, and friends). Participants schedule rides with their volunteer driver. Mileage reimbursement is provided, and volunteer training is required. The Vets Driving Vets program matches veterans who volunteer their services with other veterans who need rides to doctors' appointments or to take care of other basic needs. 2051 Kaen Road #135 Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Age 65 and older and adults with disabilities Hours: Transportation services: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 655-8208 3 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GILLIAM COUNTY SENIOR SERVICES GILLIAM COUNTY TRANSPORTATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides transportion to medical appointments, shopping, social, or business trips, with medical being the priority. 615 E Cottonwood Lane Condon, OR 97823 Eligibility: Unrestricted; age 60 and older and people with disabilities are prioritized Hours: Phone hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-5pm Transportation available Monday-Friday, and occasional weekends Email (541) 351-9545 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HEARTS WITH A MISSION HEARTS WITH A MISSION MEDFORD HEARTS FOR SENIORS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a volunteer-based companion care program for seniors, assisting with daily tasks such as housework, meal preparation, shopping, transportation to appointments, pet care, home safety, minor repairs, and yard work. The program is volunteer-driven and professionally supported. Volunteer opportunities are available. 517 Edwards Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Companion Care: For individuals aged 65 and older who live independently in their homes and are socially isolated, with no significant cognitive impairment. Volunteers: Varies per assignment Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Companion Care: Varies per assignment Email (541) 646-7385 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMPACT NW IMPACT NW SOUTHEAST MULTICULTURAL SENIOR CENTER TRANSPORTATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Assists older adults and adults with disabilities in accessing transportation. Provides escorted door-to-door rides, monthly TriMet bus and LIFT tickets for those who qualify, and group rides to major grocery stores, food banks, and recreational outings. 4610 SE Belmont Street Portland, OR 97215 Eligibility: Age 60 and older and adults with disabilities Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-4:30pm Email (503) 226-0700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details INNER COURT FAMILY CENTER PUBLIC & ODVA TRANSPORTATION PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Public transit program provides rides for everyone and also offers rides through the Oregon Department of Veterans Affairs (ODVA). 705 Chewaucan Street Paisley, OR 97636 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 407-0317 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LANE COUNCIL OF GOVERNMENTS Visit Website More Details METROPOLITAN FAMILY SERVICE METROPOLITAN FAMILY SERVICE NE 24TH AVENUE PROJECT LINKAGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides door-to-door transportation to medical appointments and errands. Project Linkage partners with Ride Connection to deliver accessible transportation to medical appointments, shopping, errands, volunteering, and recreational trips. 2200 NE 24th Avenue Portland, OR 97212 Eligibility: Age 60 and older and adults with disabilities Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 249-0471 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MORROW COUNTY PUBLIC TRANSIT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides public transit options. Eligibility: Morrow County residents Hours: Phone hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Ride hours vary by destination Email (541) 676-5667 541-676 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEIGHBORHOOD HOUSE NEIGHBORHOOD HOUSE MAIN OFFICE NEIGHBORHOOD HOUSE SENIOR SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Services include specialized information and assistance, transportation scheduling, and wellness programs that includes foot care. 8959 SW Barbur Boulevard Portland, OR 97219 Eligibility: Age 55 and older Hours: Tuesday-Friday 9am-3:30pm Email (541) 246-1663 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION TRANSIT AND TAXI SERVICE FINDER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an online search tool to help locate local bus services, shuttle services, taxi services, Dial-a-Ride, and long distance bus travel. 355 Capitol Street NE MS 11 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable Get Directions Visit Website More Details RIDE CONNECTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides transportation services for outings such as medical appointments, grocery shopping, community centers, and social visits. Lift-equipped transportation is available. Travel training on public transit is also available. Personal and group travel training is provided by a trainer or peer who accompanies the participant or group to assist them in learning to use public transportation. 9955 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: -Varies by service, primarily older adults and people with disabilities. Some programs define older adult as 65 and older. -Travel training is primarily for age 60 and older and adults who identify as having a disability Hours: Scheduling hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-5pm. Most transportation services are available Monday-Friday. Email (503) 226-0700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details URBAN LEAGUE OF PORTLAND SENIOR INFORMATION AND ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Connects older adults with services that allow them to remain safely in their homes and communities (elder law, home maintenance and minor repair services, housekeeping assistance, Medicare counseling, housing information and referral, advocacy, and senior ride program). Services include Oregon Project Independence Services (OPI) and community resources. Additionally offers short-term case management (Options Counseling) and chronic disease management. 3934 NE Martin Luther King Jr Boulevard Portland, OR 97212 Eligibility: Age 60 and older with a focus on the African-American community Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 280-2600 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS COUNTY SOCIAL SERVICES TRANSPORTATION REACHING PEOPLE GILLIAM COUNTY SENIOR SERVICES GILLIAM COUNTY TRANSPORTATION HEARTS WITH A MISSION HEARTS FOR SENIORS IMPACT NW TRANSPORTATION SERVICES INNER COURT FAMILY CENTER PUBLIC & ODVA TRANSPORTATION PROGRAMS LANE COUNCIL OF GOVERNMENTS SENIOR & DISABILITY SERVICES SENIOR CONNECTIONS LANE COUNCIL OF GOVERNMENTS SENIOR & DISABILITY SERVICES SENIOR CONNECTIONS LANE COUNCIL OF GOVERNMENTS SENIOR & DISABILITY SERVICES SENIOR CONNECTIONS LANE COUNCIL OF GOVERNMENTS SENIOR & DISABILITY SERVICES SENIOR CONNECTIONS LANE COUNCIL OF GOVERNMENTS SENIOR & DISABILITY SERVICES SENIOR CONNECTIONS LANE COUNCIL OF GOVERNMENTS SENIOR & DISABILITY SERVICES SENIOR CONNECTIONS METROPOLITAN FAMILY SERVICE PROJECT LINKAGE NEIGHBORHOOD HOUSE NEIGHBORHOOD HOUSE SENIOR SERVICES OREGON DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION TRANSIT AND TAXI SERVICE FINDER RIDE CONNECTION RIDE CONNECTION URBAN LEAGUE OF PORTLAND SENIOR INFORMATION AND ASSISTANCE
